AAPL.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Transitional / mixed-to-cautious. Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% (third consecutive hold, no cuts priced through 2026) with higher-for-longer as base case due to Iran conflict-driven energy inflation (IMF: 4.4% headline, 3.1% global growth). Dow on fifth straight losing session. Meta -9% capex shock is injecting near-term sector risk-off sentiment for mega-cap tech. S&P gained ~9% in April but intraday chop persists. Regime is NOT cleanly risk-on — macro wind is not clearly at the back of a long AAPL trade. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: AAPL's 21d ROC of +9.54% signals medium-term strength and the stock is above all EMAs (fully aligned uptrend). However, 5d ROC of -1.1% reveals a near-term stall/shallow retracement while the market is flat. RSI-14 at 60.63 is healthy, RSI-21 at 66.79 shows medium-term constructiveness, but hourly RSI at 63.34 with a bearish MACD histogram (-0.166) indicates near-term fading momentum. Volume today is sub-average (relative volume 0.73 daily, 0.32 hourly) — no institutional accumulation signal confirming sector inflows. AAPL is tracking in-line with the market, not leading. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry ~271.55, nearest resistance 276.11 (11 tests) = +1.7% upside to first target. Stop at 265.00 (below hourly support cluster at 267.20 and swing low 265.07) = -2.4% downside. Immediate R/R to first resistance: 0.71:1 — fails the 2:1 minimum. Extended target at Fib 1.272 extension of 284.00 = +4.6% upside vs. -2.4% downside = 1.92:1 — marginally below the 2:1 threshold, and reaching 284 requires clearing 276 resistance that has 11 prior tests. R/R does not meet minimum bar. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION: Balance sheet is strong (no leverage concerns at $3.99T market cap / robust FCF). ATR 2.06% is moderate and consistent with the regime. Key thesis risks per wiki: (a) iPhone margin survival in tariff regime unresolved; (b) credible AI story still absent. Wiki records three consecutive neutral-to-weak-buy reads — no new catalyst has emerged to change that. Macro headwinds (Iran risk, higher-for-longer, Meta contagion) directly conflict with the structurally bullish chart (above all MAs, EMAs aligned). Thesis conflict + sub-2:1 R/R = pass.
